title: How Anthropic Employees ACTUALLY Use Claude Skills
summary: Austin Marchese analyzes Anthropic's internal practices for Claude Skills across teams (engineering, marketing, legal). He argues Skills should not be understood merely as markdown files and outlines five lessons: understanding categories, using power components, focusing on verifiers, writing gotchas, and tuning triggers.
writeup: Austin Marchese analyzes how Anthropic employees use Claude Skills internally and distills five lessons: understanding categories, using power components, focusing on verifiers, writing gotchas, and tuning triggers. The argument is that Skills are more than markdown files, and that a well-designed skill system compounds over time. The takeaways concentrate on the structural choices behind skills rather than treating them as static prompt files.
